---
title: "2026-07-13"
---

## Analysis contract reset

The active descriptive metrics now use raw annual mean LSWT:

- long-term warming: Theil--Sen slope of raw annual mean LSWT, reported as °C per 40 years;
- annual warming speed: adjacent-year raw annual LSWT difference;
- acceleration: Sen slope of annual warming speed over 1982--2020.

STL is retained only to construct a low-frequency trajectory before PCA, with `nt=99`. PCA interprets PC1--PC5 (82.1% cumulative variance); 95% cumulative variance is a diagnostic, not a retention rule.

STARS / ST_AIS and Step 12 are retired from the active workflow. They must not re-enter the draft through historical helpers or output directories.

## Producer and rendering changes

Step 06 was extended and rerun for all 92,245 lakes. It now emits `raw_annual_mean_temp_diff_sen_slope_1e3`, the canonical raw-annual acceleration metric. The `nt=99` output has 146 columns and all lakes have finite raw acceleration values.

Chapter data preparation is now separated from composition:

- `01-global-kinematics-helpers.R` supplies Chapter 1 inputs and statistics;
- `02-warming-patterns-helpers.R` supplies Chapter 2 PCA, map, and regression data;
- qmd files no longer ingest CSVs or perform reusable joins/reshaping.

Both [Global Kinematics](../draft/01-global-kinematics.qmd) and [Warming Pattern Decomposition](../draft/02-warming-patterns.qmd) render with the updated helpers.

## Next scientific diagnostic: ice and seasonality

Potential late-period cooling or deceleration in North American and Eurasian lakes should first be tested as a seasonal diagnostic, not attributed to glacier meltwater. The immediate prediction is disproportionately weak or negative JJA trends in the relevant cohorts, evaluated alongside DJF/MAM/SON and annual ice-duration change.

A glacier-meltwater mechanism requires later glacier-catchment connectivity and inflow evidence. Geographic proximity alone is insufficient. The full protocol is in [Seasonal and Ice Diagnostics](../prose/seasonal-ice-diagnostics.qmd).

## Literature and dialogue provenance

Only `notes/papers/` is treated as the user-reviewed literature corpus. The old collection and point notes were removed because they were AI-generated aggregations rather than the user's reading notes. New reading summaries enter [Reading Queue](../../../notes/reading-queue.qmd) first and become paper notes only after review.

Use [Research Dialogue Protocol](../../../notes/research-dialogue.qmd) to distinguish brainstorming, analysis design, audit, decision, and reading-brief turns.
